DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Hong Jacqueline Nguyen Gardner's demurrer to Defendant and Cross-Complainant Sofia New's cross-complaint is overruled. The cross-complaint alleges that Gardner issued a check to New for $52,000, and that the check was rejected and returned for insufficient funds. The cross-complaint easily states facts sufficient to state a cause of action. Civil Code section 1719 provides in part that any person who draws a check that is dishonored due to insufficient funds shall be liable to the payee for the amount owing upon the check, a service charge not to exceed $25, and treble damages under certain circumstances. "Even without section 1719, a payee may sue on an instrument for the amount due on a dishonored check (UCC, Sec. 3414, subd. (b)(1).)" (Cohen v. Disner (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 855, 859; see also Imperial Merchant Services, Inc. v. Hunt (2009) 47 Cal.4th 381, 394-395 ["a merchant or other payee has the option of bypassing section 1719, if it deems the service charge to be inadequate, and of suing on the underlying obligation instead" under the Commercial Code ]; Gambord Meat Co. v. Corbari (1952) 109 Cal.App.2d 161, 162 ["When one issues a check upon a bank, there is a dual promise implied: First, that the bank upon which it is drawn will pay the amount thereof; and, second, if such bank should fail to make the payment, the drawer will pay the same to the holder thereof."]; Roff v. Crenshaw (1945) 69 Cal.App.2d 536, 541 ["There seems to be no doubt as to the right of the payee of a check, upon its nonpayment, to sue the drawer either upon the check itself or on the original consideration. . . . A check is a contract . . . ."].)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SIMON LAM AND EAST WEST BANK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' (East West Bank, N.A. and Lam) demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is sustained without leave to amend. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.10(e).)

To plead a breach of contract claim, plaintiff must allege: (1) the existence of a contract; (2) plaintiff's performance or excuse for nonperformance; (3) defendant's breach; and (4) damage to plaintiff therefrom. (Richman v. Hartley (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1182, 1186; see also CACI 303.) Here, the face of the amended complaint shows that co-defendants (the News) entered into the adduced agreement with plaintiff and there are no facts showing how East West Bank, N.A. and Lam can be liable on the agreement. (See Amended Complaint, pars. 8, 19, Ex. 1.) Moreover, there is no authority showing how the bank is liable merely because its personnel was present during the transaction and requested that funds be deposited into one of its accounts. (Amended Complaint, par. 11.) Plaintiff's reference to third-party beneficiary law does not establish a claim. If movants are intended third-party beneficiaries, then they would have the right to enforce an agreement for their benefit. (See Civ. Code 1559 ["A contract, made expressly for the benefit of a third person, may be enforced by him at any time before the parties thereto rescind it."] Plaintiff pleads no facts nor cites any authority showing how defendants as purported third-party beneficiaries assumed contractual obligations to plaintiff.

Order is signed.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MELVIN S. HODGES, Esq. (Tel: 510-838-7711) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SIMON LAM AND EAST WEST BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (East West Bank, N.A. and Lam) demurrer to the complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.

The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. To plead a breach of contract claim, plaintiff must allege: (1) the existence of a contract; (2) plaintiff's performance or excuse for nonperformance; (3) defendant's breach; and (4) damage to plaintiff therefrom. (See Richman v. Hartley (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1182, 1186; see also CACI 303.) Here, the face of the complaint shows that co-defendants (the News) entered into the adduced agreement with plaintiff and there are no facts showing how East West Bank, N.A. and Lam can be liable on the agreement. (See Complaint, pars. 8, 19, Ex. 1.) Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead a basis for contractual liability against the bank defendants, if she can do so in good faith. The allegation that the bank defendants were agents or conspirators is conclusory. (Complaint, par. 7.) Also, there is no authority showing how the bank is liable merely because its personnel was present during the transaction and requested that funds be deposited into one of its accounts. (Complaint, par. 11.) Plaintiff's reference to third party beneficiary law makes little sense. If movants are intended third party beneficiaries, then they would have the right to enforce an agreement for their benefit. (See Civ. Code sec. 1559 ["A contract, made expressly for the benefit of a third person, may be enforced by him at any time before the parties thereto rescind it."] Plaintiff pleads no facts nor cites any authority showing how defendants as third-party beneficiaries assumed contractual obligations to plaintiff. The demurrer to the breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is sustained with leave to amend because plaintiff does not plead facts showing a contractual relationship. (See Smith v. San Francisco (1990) 225 Cal.App.3d 38, 49.)

The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. When alleging fraud, the pleading must "'show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.'" (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal. 4th 631, 645.) Here, the fraud claim lacks the requisite particularity. Moreover, this claim is dubious and it is unclear how plaintiff can plead scienter given that the News started performing under the agreement and made payments to plaintiff on the note. (Complaint, par. 12.)

The demurer to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. "[T]he relationship between a bank and its depositor is not fiduciary in character." (Das v. Bank of America, N.A. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, 741.) Plaintiff has leave to plead facts showing how the parties created a fiduciary relationship. (See Zumbrun v. University of Southern California (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 1, 13 [sustaining demurrer where no facts were alleged showing fiduciary relationship].)

Defendants to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.
